sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.82 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$R$be the normal closure of an element$r$in a free group$F$with the natural length function and suppose that$s$is an element of minimal length in$R$. Is it true that$s$is conjugate to one of the following elements:$r$,$r^{-1}$,$[r,f]$,$[r^{-1},f]$for some$f F$? { V.\,N.\,Remeslennikov} No, not always (J.\,McCool, Glasgow Math.~J., { 43}, no.\,1 (2001), 123--124).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
